nodes.label: AI as ideation partner and knowledge manager
nodes.grounding_span: I use it for a back and forth ideation partner then have it sum up what we said to store it in Notion

nodes.label: AI automation for executive function support
nodes.grounding_span: I have autism and ADHD, so executive function is a real problem for me. I use AI as much as possible to automate things

nodes.label: AI's ability to produce authentic voice
nodes.label_negative: AI's generic output lacking personal touch
nodes.grounding_span: it can't add the touches that are going to make it sound like it's perfectly in my voice

nodes.label: AI efficiency for low-stakes tasks
nodes.label_negative: Time-consuming manual effort for trivial tasks
nodes.grounding_span: We did probably 25 minutes of meaningless work in 10 seconds

nodes.label: AI as skill for working with technology
nodes.grounding_span: knowing how to work WITH AI to produce something that's better quality than something that's just human made or something that's just AI made? That skill's going to be essential

nodes.label: AI as personalized external brain
nodes.label_negative: Generic AI without memory of individual
nodes.grounding_span: I allow Perplexity to store memories... I'd also love for it to serve as my external brain

nodes.label: AI designed to teach concepts
nodes.grounding_span: I really appreciate the move towards models that are designed to teach concepts, rather than just give answers

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: every minute is incredibly valuable

nodes.label: Personal authenticity and self-expression
nodes.grounding_span: make it sound like it's perfectly in my voice

nodes.label: Professional competence and future-proofing
nodes.grounding_span: That skill's going to be essential, at least for the next few years

nodes.label: Cognitive support and memory
nodes.grounding_span: serve as my external brain, allowing me to search and recall previous information

nodes.label: Educational effectiveness
nodes.grounding_span: makes it easier for me to promote AI use to other educators

nodes.label: Enthusiasm for AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I use it for a back and forth ideation partner

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of personal voice
nodes.grounding_span: it can't add the touches that are going to make it sound like it's perfectly in my voice

nodes.label: Pride in AI's efficiency for trivial tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I was strangely... proud of the AI for helping us be so efficient

nodes.label: Acceptance of AI as inevitable and need to adapt
nodes.grounding_span: I think that we have to accept AI as a given at this point, and plan with it in mind

nodes.label: Optimism about personalized AI future
nodes.grounding_span: I think we're going to wind up with one predominant AI model that we personify and that is better trained on us as individuals

nodes.label: Appreciation for concept-teaching AI models
nodes.grounding_span: I really appreciate the move towards models that are designed to teach concepts

nodes.label: Pragmatic automation-seeking — offloads routine tasks to AI
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI as much as possible to automate things

nodes.label: Iterative back-and-forth ideation — builds on AI responses
nodes.grounding_span: There's a lot of back-and-forth, with me driving the direction of the conversation but also building off what AI is giving me
